com.mentor.chs.api

Interface IXBlock

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXAbstractPinList, IXConnectivityObject, IXObject, IXPinList<IXAbstractPin>, IXWriteableObject

    public interface IXBlock
extends IXAbstractPinList
    Interface for representing the design blocks. Blocks are higher level views of designs. Users can navigate between the blocks and their associated designs. Highways routed between blocks show the interconnections between designs.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      IXDesign getAssociatedDesign(java.util.Collection<? extends IXDesign> designsInscope)
      This method returns the design revision associated to the block.
      java.util.Set<? extends IXHighway> getHighways()
      This method never returns null.

    • Method Detail

      • getHighways

        java.util.Set<? extends IXHighway> getHighways()

        This method never returns null. If there are no highways connected, it returns an empty collection.

        Example:

        The following example finds the highways common between two blocks 
         Set<IXHighway> getCommonHighways(IXBlock block1, IXBlock block2) {
        Set<IXHighway> highways1 =  new HashSet<IXHighway>(block1.getHighways());
        Set<IXHighway> highways2 =  new HashSet<IXHighway>(block2.getHighways());
        return highways1.retainAll(highways2);
 }
        Returns:
        A Set of highways connected to the block

      • getAssociatedDesign

        IXDesign getAssociatedDesign(java.util.Collection<? extends IXDesign> designsInscope)

        This method returns the design revision associated to the block. Note that block is associated to a design tree and not to a particular revision of the design. So this method takes a design scope, based on which design revision will be returned. If null is passed then design scope is picked from the active build list. If there is no active build list then latest revision of the associated design will be returned.

        Example:

        The following example finds the design associated to a block. The result of this function will vary based on the design revision in the scope of designs. 

         IXDesign getAssociatedDesignFromBuildList(IXBlock block, IXBuildList buildList) {
                Collection<IXDesign> designsInScope = new ArrayList<IXDesign>();
      designsInScope.add(childDesign1);
     //childDesign1 is one of the design revision in the Revision tree associated to block.
     IXDesign associatedDesign = block.getAssociatedDesign(buildList.getDesigns());
            return associatedDesign;
 }
        Parameters:
        designsInscope - This is scope of designs used to get the design associated to the block. If null will allow the selection of design from active build list or the latest revision of the associated design.
        Returns:
        The design revision currently associated from the list of designs passed as arguments. This will be null if block is not associated to any of the designs passed as scope or block is not associated to any design
